criteria_version: search/inclusion_criteria.md §1 (Stage-1 title-abstract screen) — priority batch 2, records 100-199 by priority score

decisions.reason: OBHDP 1994 on gain/loss framing × loss aversion × frame adoption in negotiation; eligible (negotiation outcome, framing manipulation).
J Health Comm 2010 2×2 experiment on framing × efficacy appeals × reactance for college drinking; eligible (experimental, framing, behavior-intention outcome).

Int J Advertising 2023 three lab experiments + natural setting study on concrete vs abstract advertising under uncertainty; eligible (experimental, concrete-construal framing, product persuasion outcome).

J Behavioral Decision Making 2021 framing effect × option complexity in risky/intertemporal choice; framing mechanism present but population/context is risky-choice not commercial; need full text per §2.1.

J Exp Psych Applied 2018 two experiments on self-persuasion via open-ended questions (calibrated question) in anti-alcohol media; eligible (experimental, calibrated-question technique, behavior intention outcome).
J Advertising 2018 three studies on construal-level theory + psychological distance for taboo ads (perfume, alcohol); eligible (experimental, construal manipulation, consumer-response outcomes).
Health Comm 2018 controlled experiment N=101 on framing × narrative for smoking-cessation risk perceptions; eligible.
J Advertising 2018 experiment on gain/loss framing × self/family orientation × collectivism for saving-behavior PSAs; eligible (advertising context, framing, behavior intention).
J Health Comm 2017 attention-pattern experiment on gain/loss framing for depression help-seeking; eligible (experimental, framing, help-seeking outcome).
Health Comm 2016 N=82 within-subjects 2×2×2 experiment on gain/loss × modality × repetition for weight-management reactance; eligible.
J Marketing Management 2016 experimental study on regulatory fit × service-brand activities in social media for customer engagement; eligible (experimental, regulatory fit, brand-engagement outcome).
Health Comm 2015 N=193 experiment on framing × perceived susceptibility for African American parents' HPV vaccination intent; eligible.
J Consumer Psych 2015 three experiments on regulatory fit × actor/observer visual perspective in advertising; eligible (experimental, regulatory fit, product-evaluation outcome).
JMR 2014 field experiment + online experiment on contingent match incentives × social-proof for charitable donations; eligible (field experiment, social-proof variant, donation outcome).
J Consumer Research 2013 experiments on psychological distance × construal level on affect-based evaluation; eligible (experimental, construal manipulation, consumer-evaluation outcome).
EJSP 2008 two experiments on regulatory fit × narrative engagement → persuasion; eligible (experimental, regulatory fit, persuasion outcome).
J Marketing 2005 two experiments on framing × consumer responses to product risk; eligible (experimental, framing, consumer-decision outcome).
Human Comm Research 2004 N=379 2×3 experiment on loss-gain × individual-societal framing for news cognitive complexity; eligible (framing manipulation, persuasion-relevant outcome).
Basic and Applied Soc Psych 2003 experiment on FITD × monetary incentives × labeling on homelessness petition + food drive; eligible (experimental, FITD, behavioral compliance).
J Consumer Research 1986 five experiments on anchoring-and-adjustment in spousal product-preference predictions; eligible (experimental, anchoring mechanism, consumer-decision context).
PSPB 1981 experiment on FITD with helping behavior, measuring self-perception mediation; eligible (experimental, FITD, compliance outcome).
Psychological Reports 2019 direct replication of Freedman & Fraser FITD in Poland + Ukraine across 40-50 year time gap; eligible (replication, FITD, compliance outcome).
J Health Psych 2004 RCT N=441 on gain/loss framing for Pap test utilization × prevention/detection; eligible (RCT, framing, behavior outcome).
J Business Research 1995 'Message framing and buying behavior: A field experiment'; eligible (field experiment, framing, buying-behavior outcome).

J Consumer Behaviour 2022 two field experiments on company tours as customer education tool → pro-brand behaviors; eligible (field experiments, consumer-behavior outcome — relevant to takeaway technique mechanism).

Strategic Management Journal 2021 field experiments on entrepreneur network-activation persuasion tactics for investor referrals; eligible (field experiments, persuasion tactics, commercial-investment outcome — directly relevant to closing literature).
Int J Indian Psychology 2020 experiment on FITD compliance × gender in Indian context; eligible (experimental, FITD, compliance outcome — cross-cultural extension).
Psychological Reports 2016 two experiments (N=120, N=1292) on combined DITF + but-you-are-free for non-profit donations; eligible (experimental, DITF, donation-compliance outcome).
Social Psychology 2015 experiment on mortality salience × DITF for charity compliance; eligible (experimental, DITF, compliance outcome).
Swiss J Psychology 2015 field experiment on FITD with implicit deviant request on public bench; eligible (field experiment, FITD, behavioral compliance).
Social Psych and Personality Science 2013 three experiments on construal-level mediation of DITF backfire in prosocial domain; eligible (experimental, DITF, compliance outcome).
Psychology & Marketing 2007 two experiments on framing × product-function moderator for health-care print ads; eligible (experimental, framing, advertising-persuasion outcome).
J Applied Soc Psych 2003 two experiments on gain/loss goal-framing in job ads × organizational attractiveness; eligible (experimental, framing, applicant-decision outcome).
J Applied Soc Psych 2003 three experiments on lowball + public commitment vs interruption procedure for donations; eligible (experimental, lowball, donation-compliance outcome).
Transfusion 2001 N=254 experiment on gain/loss framing × confidence in blood transfusion safety; eligible (experimental, framing, donor-related attitude/confidence outcome).
J Social Psychology 1988 N=90 experiment on FITD/DITF × spontaneous/asked-for helping; eligible (experimental, FITD/DITF, compliance outcome).
Psychological Reports 1978 N=180 telephone experiment extending FITD × effort × benefit × familiarization; eligible (experimental, FITD, compliance outcome).
JESP 2007 three studies on selective-exposure × gain/loss framing in post-decision information search; eligible (experimental, framing, post-decision behavior).
2020 conceptual replication of Tversky-Kahneman prospect theory framing in US/Netherlands pandemic policy preference survey; eligible (conceptual replication, framing, policy-preference outcome).
Electronic Markets 2020 study on AI chatbot use of FITD + commitment-consistency for e-commerce user compliance; eligible (experimental, FITD, compliance outcome in commerce context).
Public Health Nutrition 2020 experiment on gain/loss framing × audio-visual cartoon for children's eating behavior; eligible (experimental, framing, behavior outcome).
Risk Analysis 2019 N=1039 experiment on loss/gain framing × dose × reactance for physical-inactivity messages; eligible (experimental, framing, persuasion outcome).
Frontiers Communication 2018 experiment on social-proof (FB likes/comments) for organic food information; eligible (experimental, social-proof, consumer-attitude outcome).
Health Marketing Quarterly 2016 fundraising field experiment using FITD for cancer-awareness donations; eligible (field experiment, FITD, donation outcome).
K-State Research Exchange 2005 dissertation/article on FITD + DITF × commitment/consistency × reciprocity for reducing binge drinking; eligible (dissertation per §4.8, FITD/DITF mechanisms).
Korean Journal of Marketing 2021 study on price-discount framing for purchase intention; eligible (study in marketing journal, framing, purchase-intention outcome).
J Marketing Communications 2021 study on UGC as social-proof for online purchase intention; eligible (study in marketing journal, social-proof, purchase-intention outcome).
Int J Advertising 2019 three studies on text vs pictures in ads × construal level × product type for attitudes/purchase intentions; eligible (experimental, construal, purchase-intention outcome).
Communication Research 2019 meta-analysis (k=25, N=5772) on emotion-mediated gain-loss framing effect; eligible (meta-analysis of primary experimental studies).
J Health Comm 2016 online study on framing × consideration of future consequences for fruit-intake intent; eligible.

Communication Research Reports 2013 N=900 computer-mediated FITD field experiment in sales context (extreme-sports store email solicitation); eligible (field experiment, FITD, sales-context compliance outcome — directly on-thesis).
JMR 2011 three studies on mindset (concrete/abstract) × loss/gain framing for consumer recycling; eligible (experimental, framing × construal, conservation-behavior outcome).

J Health Comm 2011 interview study with 102 organ procurement coordinators on social-proof message strategies for familial donation consent; qualitative — not RCT/experimental design; need full text to judge if any quantitative component eligible.
J Marketing Communications 2005 two studies on commitment-consistency for environmental cause-related marketing; eligible (experimental, commitment-consistency, consumer-preference outcome).
PSPB 2001 studies on preference-for-consistency × FITD for compliance/non-compliance; eligible (experimental, FITD, compliance outcome).
Health Comm 1995 FITD field experiment with mall-merchant population at health fair for cancer prevention; eligible (field experiment, FITD, compliance outcome).

Basic and Applied Soc Psych 1994 3×2 experiment on persuader-side effects of using coercive vs rational influence; outcome is persuader's feelings, not target compliance — does this meet §4.4 outcome eligibility? Need full text.
Western J Communication 1994 fundraising field study on FITD × pregiving for AIDS-charity donations; eligible (field study, FITD + reciprocity, donation outcome).
Basic and Applied Soc Psych 1989 experiment on source strength × FITD/DITF compliance; eligible (experimental, FITD/DITF, compliance outcome).

JPSP 1987 FITD experiment with children (kindergarten/grade 2/grade 5) on prosocial behavior + helpful labeling; eligible (experimental, FITD, compliance outcome — child population still meets §2.1 as participants making decisions).
PSPB 1980 N=154 faculty-to-students FITD/DITF/control compliance experiment; eligible (experimental, FITD/DITF, compliance outcome).
JPSP 1979 review-as-empirical-analysis of FITD self-perception mediation; primary studies in scope; eligible (synthesis of empirical literature).
JPSP 1979 — duplicate of i=161 (same paper); flag at dedup-extraction for merge.
JMR 1977 Reingen & Kernan 2×2 factorial extending FITD to marketing-research interview-compliance context; eligible (experimental, FITD, marketing-research commercial setting).
JMR 1977 — duplicate of i=163 (same paper); flag at dedup-extraction for merge.
PSPB 1977 telephone-survey field experiment on FITD with monetary payment; eligible (field experiment, FITD, compliance outcome).
JESP 2017 on gain-loss framing in trust/reciprocity dilemmas; framing manipulation but dilemma-game population not commercial decision-makers; need full text to check §2.1.
J Advertising 2017 three studies on guilt/shame × framing for environmental ads (water conservation); eligible (experimental, framing, advertising-response outcome).
Health Comm 2012 meta-analysis (k=32, N=11,814) on gain/loss framing for vaccination; eligible (meta-analysis of primary experimental studies).
Communication Research Reports 2011 experiment on gain/loss framing for sun-safety + adolescent reactance; eligible (experimental, framing, persuasion-relevant outcome).
J Communication 2009 meta-analysis (53 studies, N=9145) on gain/loss framing for disease detection; eligible (meta-analysis).
JESP 2009 on message framing × color priming × persuasion; eligible (experimental, framing, persuasion outcome).
J Health Comm 2007 meta-analysis (93 studies, N=21,656) on gain/loss framing for disease prevention; eligible (meta-analysis).
J Health Comm 2007 commentary/methodological response to O'Keefe & Jensen meta-analysis; flag as commentary not empirical — borderline; provisional include for citation chasing the underlying meta.
Psychological Bulletin 1997 Rothman & Salovey theoretical synthesis of framing for health behavior; eligible (review of empirical literature, primary studies in scope).
JMR 1986 synthesis of FITD and DITF research with effect-size compilation; eligible (synthesis of primary empirical literature).
JPSP 1975 second indexed copy of Cialdini-tradition FITD/DITF 2x2 timing study (deduplicate flag — same as i=0).

J Exp Psych General 2022 N=1954 experiments on observers penalizing decision-makers' framing risk preferences; framing manipulation but outcome is third-party social perception of decision-makers, not the decision-makers' own compliance; need full text per §2.1/§4.4.
Health Comm 2022 2×2 N=114 experiment on gain-loss × temporal framing for social distancing; eligible (experimental, framing, behavior outcome).

J Exp Psych General 2022 two preregistered replications of tune-out effect (failure-feedback learning) × loss-framing; framing manipulation but outcome is learning from failure not commercial compliance; need full text per §2.1.

Int J Advertising 2021 two experiments on AI recommendation × perceived similarity × psychological distance for persuasion; eligible (experimental, construal, persuasion outcome in commercial setting).
J Marketing Communications 2021 N=296 between-subjects experiment on psychological distance × corporate social advocacy on consumer expectation/evaluation; eligible.
Health Comm 2020 systematic review (N=316 articles) on framing concept in health communication; eligible (review of empirical framing literature).
J Health Comm 2018 2×2×2 RCT on framing × source × social-endorsement (likes/social-proof) for Facebook health-info credibility; eligible.
J Health Comm 2018 experiment on gain/loss quit messages × ability-to-process moderator for smokers; eligible.
Polish Psychological Bulletin 2017 study on non-numerical anchoring heuristic mechanism; eligible (experimental, anchoring mechanism, judgment-decision outcome).
Health Comm 2017 N=86 experiment on framing × risk disclosure × perceived vaccine efficacy for influenza vaccination intent; eligible.

J Consumer Research 2017 seven studies on close-brand relationships insulating against physical pain; 'summary-close' false positive (matches 'close' meaning close-brand-relationship, not the closing technique); not in scope per taxonomy.
Health Comm 2016 two studies on gain-framed superordinate-identity HIV mass-comm campaign with N=858+; eligible (quasi-experimental design with three groups, framing, public-health behavior outcome).

J Applied Psychology 2016 four studies on regulatory-focus effects on novelty/creativity perception; regulatory-focus mechanism present but outcome is novelty perception not commercial persuasion; need full text per §4.4.
J Health Comm 2015 N=504 experiment on sensation-seeking × gain/loss framing for HIV-test promotion; eligible.
Journal of Marketing 2013 field experiment on customer referral programs × revenue/loyalty (defection rate 19%→7%); eligible (field experiment in commercial marketing setting, commercial outcomes).
J Health Comm 2012 study on perceptions of cessation outcomes × gain-framed smoking cessation telephone counseling; eligible (experimental, framing, behavior-change outcome).
J Consumer Research 2011 four experiments on psychological distance × price interpretation; eligible (experimental, construal, consumer-decision outcome including price).
JPSP 2010 experiments on psychological distance × negotiation interests-vs-issues focus; eligible (experimental, construal, negotiation outcome).
J Consumer Research 2008 two experiments on temporal × social distance for consumer product evaluations; eligible.
Psychological Science 2008 experiments on regulatory fit × nonverbal cues for message persuasion; eligible (experimental, regulatory fit, persuasion outcome).
Psychological Science 2006 anchoring-and-adjustment heuristic empirical paper; eligible (experimental, anchoring mechanism, judgment outcome).

J Behavioral Decision Making 2005 two experiments on financial-incentive/forewarning × judgmental anchoring (self-generated vs externally provided); eligible (experimental, anchoring, judgment outcome).
EJSP 2004 experiments on regulatory fit × motivational strength in goal pursuit; eligible (experimental, regulatory fit, behavioral outcome).
